text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived high in a pine tree. One fall day he heard a bird crying. "Oh dear," he thought. "I think I know just what's wrong!"

He flew down and saw a vulture just perched.

"Wow," said the owl. "That looks tired! What's wrong?"

"The babies," sighed the vulture. "They wouldn't come out."

Oh dear. That looked sad. "You know," said the owl. "You could maybe incubate one."

The vulture blinked. "You think?"

After just a week, the baby viper hatched.

"That's fantastic!" cried the vulture.

But then the viper wanted food.

"That's not very wise," complained the owl. "You're young."

"Not wise? I eat insects." Then the viper did.

The old owl laughed heartily. "You're quite good!"

Maybe there was something good after all.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
